title: Contact360 Ecosystem integrations and Platform productization

sections.title: Integration abstraction layer (unified interface for all CRMs)
Integration connector framework (adapter pattern, plugin system)
Integration data mapping (Contact360 schema ↔ CRM schema)
Salesforce contact & lead sync (bidirectional, conflict resolution)
Salesforce campaign integration (create campaigns, log activities)
HubSpot workflows & automation (trigger workflows from Contact360)
Gmail API integration (read, send, label management)
Email sync strategy (background worker, real-time webhooks)
AWS SES integration (send, configuration sets, events)
WhatsApp integration (via Twilio or WhatsApp Business API)
Apollo integration (email finder, phone finder, insights)
App submission process (developer guidelines, review, approval)
Managed services (dedicated account team, proactive support)
Custom feature toggles (enable/disable features per partner)
Advanced API customizations (custom endpoints, data models)
Org switching & context (user works across orgs)
Feature flags per tier (what's in free vs paid)
Usage limits per tier (contacts, campaigns, API calls)
Free tier design (viral features, upgrade paths)
Trial strategy (14-day free trial, full features)
Win/loss analysis (why customers choose us or competitors)
Partner health metrics (integration adoption, partner growth)
Ecosystem reporting (partner revenue, user base, growth)
Migration from competing CRMs (Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive)
Data mapping & transformation (schema conversion, field matching)
Historical data import (preserve history, activity logs)
Sync verification (test sync, data quality checks)
User training (training materials, webinars, office hours)
